trajectory.description: Observation: The baseline CLIP ViT-B/16 model achieved 0.3363 worst-region accuracy (Africa) after 3 epochs. The iteration took ~7.5 minutes, which is very fast. Analysis: We have plenty of time budget (9.8 hours) and compute (4x 48GB GPUs). Upgrading to a larger model (CLIP ViT-L/14) and training for more epochs (10) with a proper learning rate schedule (cosine with warmup) and weight decay should significantly improve performance. Decision: Update model.py, train.py, and predict.py to use ViT-L/14. Implement cosine LR scheduler and weight decay in train.py. Launch training with `accelerate launch --num_processes 4` to ensure all 4 GPUs are utilized, then submit for evaluation.

trajectory.description: Observation: The baseline ERM model achieved 0.4431 worst-region accuracy, but validation accuracy is much higher (0.7097), indicating poor generalization to minority regions (Africa). Analysis: To directly optimize for worst-region performance, we can implement Group Distributionally Robust Optimization (Group DRO) over the 5 geographic regions. I will update the dataset to load region labels, implement a custom GroupDROLoss that dynamically reweights the loss based on region-specific losses, and modify the validation loop to track and save the best model based on worst-region accuracy rather than overall accuracy. Decision: Update dataset.py, train.py, and launch a 15-epoch training run with Group DRO.

trajectory.description: Observation: Group DRO performed worse than the ERM baseline (0.4350 vs 0.4431) and took 2.5 hours for 15 epochs. Analysis: Group DRO might be too aggressive or unstable. A better approach for OOD generalization is strong data augmentation (RandAugment), differential learning rates (lower for backbone, higher for head), and saving the best model based on validation worst-region accuracy. To speed up training and allow a larger batch size, I will freeze the first 12 layers of the ViT-L/14 backbone. Decision: Rewrite train.py to use ERM with RandAugment, layer freezing, and differential LRs. Train for 12 epochs with batch size 128 per GPU.

trajectory.description: Observation: The previous iteration failed immediately, likely due to a syntax error or OOM from a large batch size. Analysis: I will rewrite train.py carefully to ensure correctness, reduce the batch size to 64 per GPU to prevent OOM, and use accelerate launch. I will train for 12 epochs with RandAugment, label smoothing, differential learning rates, and freeze the first 12 layers of the ViT-L/14 backbone to speed up training and regularize. Decision: Rewrite train.py, run training with accelerate, and submit for evaluation.

trajectory.description: Observation: Iteration 5 with frozen layers and RandAugment achieved 0.4288 worst-region accuracy, which is worse than the baseline ERM from Iteration 2 (0.4431). Analysis: Freezing the first 12 layers likely restricted the model's capacity to adapt to the satellite imagery domain, and RandAugment might have been too aggressive. Decision: Revert to full-model fine-tuning (unfrozen) with a batch size of 32 per GPU. Remove RandAugment but keep RandomResizedCrop and RandomHorizontalFlip. Add a Dropout layer (0.3) before the classifier to improve generalization. Use a conservative learning rate (1e-5 for backbone, 1e-4 for head) and train for 10 epochs, saving the best model based on validation worst-region accuracy.

trajectory.description: Observation: Iteration 6 achieved a new best worst-region accuracy of 0.4597, improving over the baseline. However, the validation worst-region accuracy peaked at 0.5928 and then degraded, indicating overfitting to majority regions at the expense of minority regions. Analysis: To improve OOD generalization and directly address the worst-region metric, I will implement three powerful techniques: 1) Region-Weighted Loss (Importance Weighting) using the square root of inverse region frequencies to balance the learning signal across regions. 2) Exponential Moving Average (EMA) of model weights, which is highly effective for OOD robustness. 3) Increased regularization via Dropout (0.4) and ColorJitter augmentation. Decision: Update train.py to include region weighting and EMA. Evaluate both the regular and EMA models during validation and save the absolute best one. Train for 12 epochs.

trajectory.description: Observation: Iteration 7 achieved a new best worst-region accuracy of 0.4817. The combination of Importance Weighting and EMA was highly effective. Analysis: We have 1.87 hours remaining, which is enough for exactly one more full 12-epoch training run (~1.4 hours). To squeeze out the final bit of performance, I will increase the strength of the region weighting by changing the power from 0.5 (square root) to 0.75, which will give a stronger penalty for errors on minority regions like Africa and Oceania. I will also add RandomVerticalFlip to the augmentations, as satellite imagery is rotation-invariant and this provides 2x more spatial variation without risking the artifacts of RandAugment. Decision: Update train.py with stronger region weights and RandomVerticalFlip, train for 12 epochs, and submit the final evaluation.
